purpose: Manages archived dashboard snapshots in the top-level Archive/ directory. Provides three operations: listArchives() returns a sorted (newest-first by name) array of archive metadata derived from each archive's initialization.json (task name + agent count); archiveDashboard(id) copies the entire dashboard directory (including progress/, logs.json, initialization.json) into Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{taskName} via a recursive copyDirSync borrowed from DashboardService; deleteArchive(name) recursively removes the named archive folder. The archive name format YYYY-MM-DD_taskName is the de facto identifier and the basis for newest-first sorting via localeCompare.
gotchas: Archive name collisions: if two swarms share the same task.name and are archived on the same day, archiveDashboard overwrites silently — copyDirSync runs mkdirSync({recursive:true}) and then copyFileSync, which clobbers same-named files but does not pre-clear extra files left from a prior archive of the same name (orphaned progress/*.json from a previous archive will linger).

readDashboardInit is lazy-required inside archiveDashboard to avoid a circular dependency with DashboardService — moving this require to the top would break module load (DashboardService imports nothing from here, but maintainers should not 'clean up' this require).
task.name is taken verbatim into the folder name with no sanitization — slashes, colons, or unicode in task.name will produce invalid or surprising paths.

Sorting is by archive name string (localeCompare), so ordering only matches chronological order because the YYYY-MM-DD prefix is lexicographically equal to date order — if the prefix format ever changes, sorting silently breaks.
deleteArchive uses fs.rmSync({recursive:true,force:true}) — irrecoverable; no soft-delete or trash.
patterns: Single source-of-truth for archive directory layout: ARCHIVE_DIR constant from utils/constants is the only place the path is defined.
Defensive try/catch wrapping listArchives so missing/unreadable Archive dir returns [] rather than throwing into route handlers.
Lazy require inside function body (`require('./DashboardService')`) used as a circular-dep-avoidance pattern.
conventions: All exported functions are named, no class wrapper.
Returns boolean from delete operation, returns string id from create operation, returns array from list operation — same convention as DashboardService.
Date stamping uses `new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` — UTC date, not local.
